highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 4.151.0 ports hot-path colour conversions to managed code and updates WASM exception handling.
highlights_body: SKColor-to-SKColorF and SKPMColor premultiply/unpremultiply now run entirely in managed integer math, eliminating P/Invoke overhead on these common paths. WASM support is refreshed with Emscripten 5.0.6 and the new exnref exception-handling model.

bullets.detail: Picks up the latest round of upstream Skia chrome/m151 fixes, keeping the bundled engine aligned with Chrome milestone 151.
Restores SK_ENABLE_LEGACY_SHADERCONTEXT so CPU-rasterised surfaces produce correct output after the m151 sync.

bullets.lead: SKColor and SKColorF conversion ported to managed code
bullets.detail: SKColor-to-SKColorF conversion is now pure managed C# integer math, removing the P/Invoke round-trip on every colour conversion call.

bullets.lead: SKPMColor premultiply/unpremultiply ported to managed code
bullets.detail: Single-value premultiply and unpremultiply operations are now handled entirely in managed integer math, avoiding native interop overhead.

bullets.lead: WASM updated to Emscripten 5.0.6 with exnref exception handling
bullets.detail: Rebuilds the WASM native libraries with Emscripten 5.0.6 and enables the new exnref-based exception handling model required by upcoming .NET WASM runtimes.
A CI test job now validates SkiaSharp against the preview .NET WASM runtime, catching exception-handling regressions before they reach stable releases.
